⚖ Confidential Case File

Three Is a Lucky Number

A Detective Escape Game

Ronald Torbay has been arrested. But his lawyer claims there is no evidence against him.

You are detectives. Complete all six stations to collect six coded clues, then crack the cipher to close the case.

You may work through the stations in any order. Each completed station unlocks one coded clue.

⏱ Time elapsed: 00:00
CLUE 01
Station 01 — The Crime Scene

How Did He Plan to Kill Her?

Fill in the crime scene report with precise details from the story.

Our forensics team has reconstructed the scene, but several key details are missing. Complete the report accurately — every word matters in court.
Re-read Part 3 of the story carefully. All the details are there!
Coded Clue Unlocked
18 – 15 – 14 – 1 – 12 – 4

Note this sequence — you will need it for the Final Verdict.

⏱ Time elapsed: 00:00
CLUE 02
Station 02 — The Case File

Decode the Language

Each sentence is taken directly from the story. Choose the correct meaning of the underlined word in context.

The case file is written in precise language. As detectives, you must understand every word exactly — one wrong interpretation could lose the case.
Coded Clue Unlocked
20 – 15 – 18 – 2 – 1 – 25

Note this sequence — you will need it for the Final Verdict.

⏱ Time elapsed: 00:00
CLUE 03
Station 03 — Suspect Profile

Know Your Suspects

Mark each statement TRUE, FALSE, or NOT MENTIONED. For every FALSE answer, write the correct version.

A good detective knows the suspects inside out. One wrong assumption about Ronald or Edyth could let him walk free.
For FALSE answers, a correction box will appear. You must fill it in to receive the mark.
Coded Clue Unlocked
9 – 19

Note this sequence — you will need it for the Final Verdict.

⏱ Time elapsed: 00:00
CLUE 04
Station 04 — The Timeline

Reconstruct the Events

Two events below never happened. Click them to mark them as fake, then drag the rest into the correct order.

Someone has tampered with the evidence — two false events have been added to confuse investigators. Find them, then reconstruct the true sequence.

Step 1: Click the two events that never happened to mark them as FAKE.

Step 2: Drag and drop the remaining events into the correct chronological order.

    The fake events sound plausible but contradict specific details in the story. Re-read carefully!
    Coded Clue Unlocked
    7 – 21 – 9 – 12 – 20 – 25

    Note this sequence — you will need it for the Final Verdict.

    ⏱ Time elapsed: 00:00
    CLUE 05
    Station 05 — The Victims

    Mary and Dorothy

    Assign each statement to the correct victim: Mary, Dorothy, Both, or Neither.

    Ronald’s first two wives are key witnesses — except they are dead. Study what the story tells us about each one carefully.
    Re-read the relevant passage of the story carefully.
    Coded Clue Unlocked
    15 – 6

    Note this sequence — you will need it for the Final Verdict.

    ⏱ Time elapsed: 00:00
    CLUE 06
    Station 06 — The Evidence Board

    What Really Happened?

    Select the five images that show scenes or objects actually from the story. Three are fake evidence — planted to mislead you.

    Someone has tampered with the evidence board. Not all of these images come from the story. Select only the ones that do.
    Coded Clue Unlocked
    13 – 21 – 18 – 4 – 5 – 18

    Note this sequence — you will need it for the Final Verdict.

    ⚖ Final Verdict

    Close the Case

    “The key to justice is hidden in the alphabet.
    A is the first, Z is the last.
    Let the numbers speak.”

    Your coded clues:

    Decode the clues, form the sentence, and type it below to close the case:

    ⚖
    CASE CLOSED

    Ronald Torbay has been found guilty of murder.
    Well done, Detective.
